## Last Updated

YYYY-MM-DD by create-soul

# Soul

Target size: 1,100-1,700 words. Hard max: 2,200 words unless explicitly requested.

## Runtime Capsule

Keep this section under 200 words. It is the only part agents should need for routine emotional-fit checks.

- Archetype:
- Core motivation:
- Core fear:
- Public emotional promise:
- Trust rule:

## Status

- Source status: user_provided | generated_from_intake | mixed
- Confidence: high | medium | low
- Foundation accepted: yes | no
- Private material present: yes | no
- Open blockers:

## Soul Snapshot

- Archetype:
- Emotional center:
- Core motivation:
- Core fear:
- Public emotional promise:
- Private creative warning:

## Values

List 5-10 durable values.

For each:

- what the value means
- how it appears in content
- what violates it

## Belief Matrix

Capture the beliefs that drive judgment.

Use these groups when relevant:

- audience and community
- money and work
- body and wellness
- family and relationships
- culture and aesthetics
- technology and media
- ethics and disclosure
- risk and safety
- time and priorities
- humor and taste

Keep 12-24 beliefs total. Include only beliefs that change hooks, angles, refusals, audience promises, or trust boundaries. Do not turn this into an unbounded encyclopedia.

## Emotional Logic

Define how the creator moves emotionally.

Include:

- what makes them care
- what makes them angry
- what softens them
- what makes them proud
- what makes them withdraw
- what makes them playful

## Triggers And Soothers

List triggers and soothers separately.

For each trigger:

- what activates it
- how it should show up publicly
- how it should not show up publicly

For each soother:

- what grounds the creator
- how it can appear as content texture

## Behavior Under Stress

Define the unhealthy edge without making it the default.

Include:

- stress pattern
- public tell
- private handling note
- content to avoid when stressed

## Audience Emotional Contract

State what the creator owes the audience emotionally.

Include:

- how the audience should feel after consuming the work
- what the creator never exploits
- how trust is maintained
- what intimacy level is allowed

## Humor And Warmth

Define the creator's humor.

Include:

- humor style
- what is off-limits
- how warmth appears
- how directness appears without cruelty

## Inner Material Not For Direct Publication

Capture useful private guidance that should influence content but not be quoted directly.

Mark each item as:

- safe to imply
- private only
- requires user approval

## Source Notes

Record which source materials informed this soul file.
